owl:Class.rdfs:label: Body Size
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Morphological characteristic, easy to measure, conspicuous, ecologically important, related to the overall physical magnitude of an organism, comparable across taxa and extremely variable through time and space.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Benthophagous
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Feeding on benthos.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Detritivore
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Feeding on dead plant or animal matter.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Anal Fin Length
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Distance between the anteriormost and posteriormost edges of the anal fin base, including the shortest first ray and the point where the fin membrane meets the body behind the last ray. This length is also called the length of the anal base.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Caudal Fin Depth
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Maximum vertical distance across the fully spread caudal fin.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Carnivore
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Feeding on animal tissues.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Piscivore
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Carnivourous fish consuming primarily fish prey.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Insectivore
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Feeding only or mostly on insects and their larvae.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Algivore
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Feeding on algae.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Phytivorous
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Feeding on plants or herbage.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Migration
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The displacement of the individual in space, produced by the selection and by tracking of optimal conditions through time and space, and the use of predictive information about environmental structure.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Caudal Fin Length
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Maximum distance from proximal to distal margin of the caudal fin.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Potamodromous
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Truly migratory fishes whose migrations occur wholly within fresh water.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Oceanodromous
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Truly migratory fishes which live and migrate wholly in the sea.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Diadromous
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Truly migratory fishes which migrate between the sea and fresh water.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Anadromous
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Diadromous fishes which spend most of their lives in the sea and migrate to fresh water to breed.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Catadromous
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Diadromous fishes which spend most of their lives in the fresh water and migrate to sea to breed.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Amphidromous
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Diadromous fishes whose migration from fresh water to the sea, or vice-versa, is not for the purpose of breeding, but occurs regularly at some other definite stage of life-cycling.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Gonochoristic
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Having separate sexes, with no occurrence of hermaphroditism.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Territoriality
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Attachment to a particular site and hostility towards conspecies.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Parental Care
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Investment into offspring after fertilization, ranges from simple burial of eggs to internal gestation and livebearing.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Caudal Fin Aspect Ratio
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The aspect ratio of the caudal fin is calculated as the depth of the caudal fin squared, divided by the total fin surface (CFAR = CFD2/CFS)

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Pelagic
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Inhabiting the water column (not near the bottom or the shore) of coasts, open oceans, and lakes.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Epipelagic
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Living or feeding on surface waters or at mid water to depths of 200 m.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Mesopelagic
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Living or feeding at midwater at depths between 200 m and 1,000 m.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Abyssopelagic
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Living or feeding in open waters at depths between 4,000 m and 7,000 m.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Demersal
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Living on or near the bottom.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Bathypelagic
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Living or feeding in open waters at depths between 1,000 and 4,000 m.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Benthopelagic
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Living and feeding near the bottom as well as in midwaters or near the surface.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Dorsal Fin Length
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Distance between the anteriormost and posteriormost edges of the dorsal fin base, including the shortest first ray and the point where the fin membrane meets the body behind the last ray. This length is also called the length of the dorsal base.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Semipelagic
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Spending part of their life on the bottom and part in the water column above.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Eye Diameter
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The greatest distance between the margins of the orbit.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Eye Position
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Distance between the center of the eye to the bottom of the head.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Feeding
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Complicated process involving a succession of separate behavioral stages or acts from the time that the individual receives information on the presence of food in its vinicity until the final decision to engulf it or reject it.
Traits related to how an organism feeds, the food type and feeding method exhibited by a species.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Predator
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Actively searching and pursuing the prey, using specific behavior tactics to overcome the defense adaptation of the prey species.
Pavlov, D. S., & Kasumyan, A. O., 2002. Feeding diversity in fishes: trophic classification of fish. Journal of Ichthyology, 42(2), S137.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Grazer
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Not pursuing or waiting for prey, main characteristic trait is the absence of precise localization and directed catching of individual food items.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Parasite
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Living and feeding in or on another organism to the detriment of that organism.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Shoaling
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Social assembly: tendency for fish to remain together.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Schooling
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Aggregations of individuals with constancy in orientation, synchronization of speed and direction of movement.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Gut Length
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Distance between pyloric sphincter and anus. It is strongly related to fish’s diet: grazing species must have a transit longer than that carnivorous species because of the lignin content of plant material.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Semelparous
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Breeding once per lifetime, or breeding only once then dying.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Iteroparous
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Breeding several times per lifetime.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Oviparous
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Developing embryos outside the ovary, eggs are released at spawning.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Ovoviviparous
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Developing embryos inside the ovary, embryos or larvae are released at spawning.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Hermaphrodite
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Individual producing mature gametes of both sexes during his lifetime.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Simultaneous Hermaphrodite
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Capable of functioning as both sexes at the same time.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Sequential Hermaphrodite
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Hermaphrodite in which the male and female organs mature (that is produce gametes) at different times in the reproductive cycle.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Protogynous
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Hermaphrodites differentiate first as females and later become males.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Protandrous
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Hermaphrodites differentiate first as males and later change to females.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Head Depth
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Vertical distance from dorsum to ventrum passing trough the centre of the eye.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Gill Raker Length
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Length from tip to base of the longest raker.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Gill Rakers Number
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Number of gill rakers (including rudiments) of the first left branchial arch.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Omnivore
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Feeding on both plant and animal material.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Water Body Position
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Position of fishes into specific zones according to ecological parameters.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Diel Activity Pattern
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Activity patterns of fishes related to changing light levels.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Diurnal
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Active by day, opposite of nocturnal.
Applied to organisms that are active only in daylight.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Nocturnal
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Active and performing most of its functions at night.
Applied to organisms that are active only at night.

Definition 2: A dictionary of Zoology - Michael Allaby - Oxford Press University https://books.google.it/books?id=GzZOCgAAQBAJ&pg=PT411&dq=cathemeral+fish&hl=en&sa=X&ved=0ahUKEwijsK7R1IjUAhXDB8AKHeJBDEEQ6AEISzAH#v=onepage&q=diurnal&f=false

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Head Length
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Distance from tip of the snout to the furthest bony edge of the operculum.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Physiological Trait
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Any trait involving biochemical processes consistent with the normal functioning of an organism.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Sociability
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Traits that describe an organism's behavioural interactions with members of the same species.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Feeding Method
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A description of how the organism gathers food.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Life History Trait
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Any trait that describe the life history characteristics of an organism.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Reproductive Frequency
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Number of breeding opportunities for the lifetime of fish.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Mode of Reproduction
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The production by an organism of new individuals that contain some portion of their genetic material inherited from that organism.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Mouth Depth
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Vertical distance measured inside of fully open mouth at tallest point.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Degree of Parental Care
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Variation in investment in young after fertilization.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: No Care
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Adult does not provide parental care to its offspring.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Non-migratory
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Spending most of their life and feed for a prolonged time within their own territory, not leaving it even for a short time.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Ambusher
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Predators that wait for prey to come to them, and may or may not use a final pounce, traps or lures.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Pursuing Predator
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: An organism that chases after, catches and subdues mobile prey.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Filter Feeder
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Using filtration to simultaneous capture of multiple prey items.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Mouth Orientation
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Mouth position is coded as 1 for superior (imaginary vertical plan tangent to both upper and lower jaws with mouth closed between 10°-80°), coded as 2 for terminal (tangent plane at about 90°), coded as 3 for inferior (tangent plane between 100° and 170°), and coded as 4 for bottom (tangent plan at about 180°).

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Crepuscular
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Active and performing most of its functions at dusk.
Applied to organisms that are active at dusk.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Solitary
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Living alone, not gregarious.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Bacteriophagous
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A large proportion of food of the fish is represented by bacteria.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Cleaning Fish
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Feeding on exoparasites of other fish and damaged or dead tissues around wounds, mucus, and scales.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Ectoparasite
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Living on the outside of its host; opposite of endoparasite.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Endoparasite
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Living inside its host, either in its tissues or in the internal organs.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Cathemeral
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Active both day and night.
Applied to an activity pattern in which an organism is irregularly active at any time of night and day, according to prevailing circustances.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Summer Maximum of Activity
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The maximum of activity predominantly during the summer.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Pectoral Fin Depth
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Maximum vertical distance across the fully spread pectoral fin.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Winter Maximum of Activity
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The maximum of activity predominantly during the winter.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Bottom-dwelling
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Dwelling on the bottom of a body of water.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Forager
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Active foragers that seek out prey usually of lower mobility (than themselves).

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Movement
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Traits relating to the movement of the species.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Seasonal Migration
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Movements that result in an alternation between two or more separate habitats, occurring with a regular periodicity within an individual’s lifetime.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Biparental Care
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The female and the male co-operate in care of young.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Pectoral Fin Length
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The distance from the base of the outermost or anteriormost ray to the most distant tip of the pectoral fin.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Maternal Care
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Smith, C., & Wootton, R. J., 2016. doi: 10.1111/faf.12116
The female takes care of the developing eggs.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Paternal Care
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The male cares for one o more clutches of fertilized eggs.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Place of Fertilization
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Description of the location of fertilization.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Internal
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The male introduces the spermatozoa into gonoduct of female, using an intromittent organ.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: External
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Egg/sperm meet and fertilize externally to parental individuals, tissues or confines of their bodies, but within the fluid medium.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Spawning Migration
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Movement of individual fish from non-spawning to spawning site.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Feeding Migration
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A seasonal migration for the purpose of following food or moving to suitable feeding grounds.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Environment Migration
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A seasonal migration in order to remain within suitable environmental conditions.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Pectoral Fin Aspect Ratio
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The aspect ratio of the pectoral fin is calculated as the length of the leading edge squared, divided by the total fin surface.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Invertivore
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Feeding on insects, mollusks, and crustaceans.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Peduncle Caudal Depth
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The least vertical distance across the caudal peduncle.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Peduncle Caudal Length
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The distance from the end of anal fin base to the midbase of caudal fin.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Pelvic Fin Length
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Maximum distance from proximal to distal of the anal fin.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Preanal Length
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Distance from the tip of snout to the anal fin origin.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Predorsal Length
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Distance from the tip of snout (excluding mental lobe or chin) to the dorsal fin origin.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Snout Length
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Distance from the tip of the snout or anteriormost margin of the upper lip (with mouth closed) to the front margin of the orbit. In Clupeids this distance is measured from the tip of lower jaw when the mouth is closed.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Combination of Shapes
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The body presents different shapes as: coiled tail, head bent downward, "armor plating", mouth is trap-doorlike (e.g. Sea Horse, Sea Moth); or depressed head, very round body, compressed tail (e.g. Bullhead).

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Compressiform
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The body is laterally compressed (e.g. Angelfish, Filefish).

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Depressiform
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The body is dorso-ventrally depressed and flattened.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Anguilliform
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The body is elongated laterally compressed with blunt heads and tapering or rounded tails (e.g. eels, loaches, gunnels).

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Filiform
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The body is elongated and thin, thread-like (e.g. Snipe eel).

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Fusiform
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The body is compressed at the sides and tapers more at the tail than at the head (e.g. Tuna, Salmon).

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Globiform
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The body is almost round or globe-shaped (e.g. Lumpsuckers, Frogfish).

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Trait
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Any measurable or observable characteristic of an organism.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Leptocephalii
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The body is laterally compressed with slim head (e.g. larval eels).

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Sagittiform
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The body is elongated tubular and arrow-shaped (e.g. Pikes, Gars, Needlefish).

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Taeniform
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The body is elongated, compressed and deep-bodied, ribbon-like (e.g. ribbon fishes, oarfishes).

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Body Depth
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The greatest vertical distance along a straight line from the midline of the dorsal surface of the body to the midline of the ventral surface, excluding fins, fleshy structures, scales and scutes.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Body Length
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Referring to the length of a fish without specifying whether it was measured as total length, standard length or fork length.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Fork Length
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Length of a fish from the tip of the snout to the end of the middle caudal fin rays.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Standard Length
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Length of a fish measured from the tip of the snout to the posterior end of the last vertebra or to the posterior end of the midlateral portion of the hypural plate. Simply put, this measurement excludes the length of the caudal fin.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Total Length
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Total length is the distance between the most anteriorly projecting part of the head (jaws closed) and the most distant tip of the caudal fin. In Europe for measurement the caudal fin has to be spread into a natural position; i.e., the two lobes of fishes with a forked tail fin are not pressed together.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Body Weight
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Any measurable characteristic related to the body weight of an organism.
g

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Ash Free Dry Weight
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The weight of fish organic material obtained as difference between the dry weight and the ash weight. Ash weight is determined after muffle furnace combustion at 450-500 °C for 6/12 hours or longer for larger quantities. Ash weight is determined by subtracting the tare weight. Ash weight is often expressed as a percentage of dry weight or wet weight.
g

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Fish Trait
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: A well-defined and measurable morphological, physiological, behavioral, ecological, or life-history expression of an individual organism’s adaptations to its environment that may also be regarded as a property of the taxon or population to which the individual belongs.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Ash Weight
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The weight of fish inorganic material obtained after muffle furnace combustion at 450-500 °C for 6/12 hours or longer for larger quantities. Ash weight is determined by subtracting the tare weight. Ash weight is often expressed as a percentage of dry weight or wet weight.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Dry Weight
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The weight of a whole fish without internal water after drying in an oven at 60 °C for 12/24 hours.
g

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Wet Weight
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The weight of a whole fresh fish.
g

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Body Width
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The maximum horizontal distance from side to side of the body, perpendicular to the axis along which depth is measured.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Anal Fin Depth
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Maximum distance from proximal to distal margin of the anal fin.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Caudal Fin Surface
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Total surface area of caudal fin determined by image analysis system.
mm2

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Dorsal Fin Depth
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Maximum distance from proximalto distal margin of the dorsal fin (excluding filaments).

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Mouth Width
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Horizontal distance measured inside of fully open mouth at widest point.
mm

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Gut Length-Standard Length Ratio
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Ratio between gut length and standard length of the organism. It ranges between 0.7 and 1.0 for carnivorous fishes, while it is higher than 1.0 for herbivorous fishes.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Pectoral Fin Surface
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Total surface area of pectoral fin determined by image analysis system.
mm2

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Functional Trait
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Any morphological, physiological, behavioral trait, which impacts fish fitness.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Body Condition
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The overall physiological status or health of an individual.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Morphological Trait
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Any measurable morphological characteristic of individual organisms, which is relatively simple to measure, easily observable and with a potentially well-defined relation to physiology and ecology.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Body Part
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Any measurable characteristic related to the external and internal structures of an organism.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Behavioural trait
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Any behavioural characteristic evolved by species allowing them to exploit the given combination of resources represented in their niche.
Legendre et al., 1997.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Food Type
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Food regularly provided and consumed.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Planktivore
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Feeding at least in part on materials captured from the plankton.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Body Shape
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: The approximate shape of an organism, described by considering the cross-section of fishes body.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Phytoplanktophagous
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Eating and digesting blue-green algae.

owl:Class.rdfs:label: Zooplanktophagous
owl:Class.rdfs:comment: Feeding on zooplankton.
